title: engines/ → project/ — mod/segments/text-ast/hostile-batteries cut destiny under decisions.md
privilege: Architect cut contract only. decisions.md is sole Spec authority — this file yields on conflict. Current condemned paths appear only as Delete evidence; every seat and rewire destination is a target-tree path. Claim-tag inflation or jointly impossible meters void this plan.

scope.this_cut: Condemned: crates/kyzo-core/src/engines/{mod,segments}.rs, engines/text/ast.rs, engines/{gazetteer_hostile,sparse_hostile}.rs — the engines/ directory and engines/text/ die. Living targets: project/{contract,residency,current,gazetteer}.rs, project/sparse/sparse_hostile.rs, project/text/ast.rs, kyzo-model/src/parse/search.rs (FTS AST pure-data half), kyzo-trials/src/fuzz.rs + kyzo-core benches (the two #[ignore]d probes re-laned).
scope.not_this_json: The individual engines (fts, hnsw, lsh, sparse, spatial, gazetteer production halves) already relocated in prior executed cuts. query/ (01), runtime/ (02), storage/ (03), data+fixed_rule (05), hosts (06), storage seats (07).
foundation_already_true: project/{contract,residency,current}.rs and project/text/ast.rs and project/sparse/sparse_hostile.rs are 1-line stub seat reservations
project/gazetteer.rs landed (874 lines — the production engine already migrated)
kyzo-model has no parse/ zone yet — kyzo-model/src/parse/search.rs is a planned map seat
scope.target_not_current: by_target and allowlist.targets are target-tree paths only.

authority: This file — cut destiny; subordinate to decisions.md

claim_tags.law: Mandatory Spec tags from decisions.md only. Every unrepresentable re-tags under exactly one. Inflating Unexposed to Unconstructible voids this plan.
claim_tags.Unconstructible: No path through the sealed Rust type and crate boundary constructs the illegal state. Proof: private constructor + compile-fail a crate-consumer trybuild can demonstrate.
claim_tags.Refused: Reachable program; typed runtime outcome. Named refuse variants.
claim_tags.Unexposed: Absent from the public API but reachable by a hostile adapter. Name residual risk; real detection or residual accepted.

joint_satisfaction.law: Allowlist ⊇ every TARGET file the meters require to change. Condemned paths are deleted via delete_after — not edited as living seats.
joint_satisfaction.proof_pairing: compile-fail for signature-enforced laws (witness-after-snapshot); grep-delete for path/symbol absence.
joint_satisfaction.no_condemned_rewire: engines/* paths appear only under deletes/delete_after.

deletes.law: E-action ledger for condemned CURRENT paths.

paths.why: Module tree dissolves into project/; the one owned concept — IndexRowCorrupt + index_rows — → project/contract.rs. The per-module #[allow(dead_code)] liveness ledger is condemned: the mod-file-as-status-board pattern dies with the monolith layout.

Validity discipline (Watermark, witness_after_snapshot, bump_before_commit, should_build + miss map) → project/residency.rs; segment structure + cache/serving (Segment, SegmentEngine, Segments context, checked_row_end) → project/current.rs. Tests split with their halves; the #82 regression battery travels with the gate.

SPLIT compelled by the crate wall: pure-data half (FtsExpr/FtsLiteral/FtsNear + flatten/is_empty + depth invariant) → kyzo-model/src/parse/search.rs; analyzer-coupled tokenize rewrite → project/text/ast.rs as an extension over the model type. engines/text/ dies with it.
Absorbed into project/sparse/sparse_hostile.rs as a NAMED hostile-review section beside the module tests. Independence lives in independently-derived references, not file separation.

Absorbed into project/gazetteer.rs's test submodule as a named hostile-review section; the two #[ignore]d heavyweight probes re-lane (corrupt-dictionary sweep → kyzo-trials/src/fuzz.rs; phase-timing scaling probe → bench lane).

symbols.name: per-module #[allow(dead_code)] liveness ledger in a mod file
symbols.why: In the target each projection lands with its surface or doesn't land; grep-guard the status-board pattern out of project/mod.rs.

delete_guarded_invariants.name: IndexRowCorrupt defined once
delete_guarded_invariants.close: rg 'IndexRowCorrupt' crates/kyzo-core/src → definition only in project/contract.rs

not_deletes.why: Nothing is condemned in either; every test crosses with its independence artifacts (f64 oracle, hand-computed bit patterns, structurally-different leftmost-longest reference).
Travels with the rebuild gate to project/residency.rs — never weakened.

refused.law: Typed runtime outcomes. Named variants. Authority honest.

items.name: undecodable index row during a projection scan
items.variant: IndexRowCorrupt (typed Diagnostic; codec refusals become the index's OWN corruption by downcast; storage/IO errors pass through unchanged)
items.authority: decisions.md §51 — projections are rebuildable cache; disagreement → rebuild or typed refuse, never silent serve
items.why: Its help text — 'the index can be dropped and re-created from its base relation' — IS the projection law; a raw DecodeError cannot leak out of an engine as its contract.

items.name: segment build past the u32 offset ceiling
items.variant: build declines (typed) — projection is optional speed
items.why: Declining-is-always-sound: the fallback pays no more than the build would have; the F7 total cast is factored out so a unit test pins the boundary arithmetic without 4.3 billion values.

unexposed.law: Residual hostile-adapter risk with real detection or residual accepted.

items.name: projection served stale by an adapter bypassing the witness gate
items.residual_risk: An in-crate caller could hand get() a witness it did not take from the open snapshot, serving a cut the base has moved past.
items.detection: the #75 join-probe twin and segments-law end-to-end battery (fresh-never-dirty through build/orphan/rollback) catch the reachable shapes; miss-map loss test proves the cache is never a source of truth; residual accepted for hostile in-crate forgery

carried_obligations.obligation: The process-local Watermark is sound ONLY while segments are memory-only. If projections ever persist, the generation vocabulary must become durable — and §20 rules what it becomes: Catalog generation is a Store commit-order position, never a second watermark organ; §35 requires two-axis coordinates on any durable projection keyspace. Name this at project/residency.rs on day one.

The k=0 check-before-push regression was fixed in BOTH the sparse engine and the identical shape in fts_search; the pin exists only on the sparse side. The fix was shared; the pin must be too — land the twin beside project/text/fts.rs.
The two #[ignore]d gazetteer probes re-lane: corrupt-dictionary sweep (incl. 2 MiB surface) → kyzo-trials/src/fuzz.rs; phase-timing scaling probe → the bench lane. Ignored unit tests are not a lane.
Segments::OFF threading is door plumbing the #120 operator wiring replaces — carry the note; do not entrench the threading as design.

tests.law: Hostile batteries land as NAMED hostile-review sections beside what they attack; independence lives in independently-derived references, not file separation.

fate.to: project/residency.rs tests (witness equality governs service; stable-miss-streak gate; #82 alternating-writes never crossing the gate; miss-map loss only delays rebuild) + project/current.rs tests (prefix ranges vs linear-scan oracle; orphan + evict + held Arc; empty segment; u32 boundary)
project/sparse/sparse_hostile.rs as a named hostile-review section
project/gazetteer.rs test submodule (named hostile-review section); corrupt sweep → kyzo-trials/src/fuzz.rs; scaling probe → bench lane
kyzo-model parse/search.rs tests (is_empty edges, flatten collapse laws) + project/text/ast.rs tests (analyzer rewrites incl. stopword-vanishing and Near distance preservation)

meters: rg -n 'summation_order|run_bits' crates/kyzo-core/src/project/sparse → battery present with exact-bit assertions
rg -n 'to_lowercase' crates/kyzo-core/src/project/gazetteer.rs → mutation-differential (İNDEX mutant kill) present

authoritative_constructs: IndexRowCorrupt + index_rows — project/contract.rs (defined ONCE because all engines name it)
Watermark + witness_after_snapshot + bump_before_commit + should_build + miss map — project/residency.rs
Segment + SegmentEngine + Segments context + checked_row_end — project/current.rs
FtsExpr + FtsLiteral + FtsNear + flatten/is_empty + depth invariant — kyzo-model/src/parse/search.rs

unconstructible.name: the racy witness order (witness taken before the snapshot it claims)
unconstructible.proof: compile-fail by signature: witness_after_snapshot takes the open snapshot BY SIGNATURE, so the racy order does not type-check — soundness by signature, not calling convention (same mechanism as the storage layer's stamp_after_snapshot)

unconstructible.name: an FtsExpr deep enough to overflow a derived recursive walk
unconstructible.proof: compile-fail shape: the parser is the only non-test constructor and bounds depth/operator count; a new public constructor for a crate consumer does not exist — every recursive walk INCLUDING compiler-derived Drop/Clone/PartialEq/Hash is stack-safe BECAUSE the bound holds

rulings.a: project/contract.rs is §51's seat in current law: projections are rebuildable cache, never WAL-authoritative meaning; on disagreement rebuild or refuse typed; silent serve is deleted. IndexRowCorrupt's downcast discipline (codec corruption owned; storage/IO passed through) is that law's read-boundary half.

kyzo-model/src/parse/search.rs must NAME the AST it produces and kyzo-model cannot depend on the engine — so the pure-data FTS AST lives model-side; project/text/ast.rs holds only the analyzer-coupled tokenize as an extension over the model type. PREFIX literals pass through whole: filtering or stemming a prefix pattern would change what it means.

Absorption does not dilute independence: the sparse battery's f64 oracle and hand-computed bit patterns, and the gazetteer's structurally-different enumeration+resumption reference (agreement between three independent derivations), are the independence — file separation never was.

Fixture plumbing follows each battery; where the target grows a shared projection-test rig it dissolves there instead of duplicating per engine — the rig, if minted, seats beside project/contract.rs tests.

ban: Do not weaken the exact-BIT score determinism standard (byte-identity, not tolerance).
Do not re-create a mod-file dead-code status board in project/mod.rs.
Do not give kyzo-model's FTS AST a public constructor that skips the parser's depth bound.
Do not let project/text own the FtsExpr type (model owns it; project extends).
Do not leave the k=0 pin one-sided (carried obligation: twin beside fts_search).
Do not keep heavyweight probes as #[ignore]d unit tests — re-lane them.
Do not serve a projection on anything but witness equality.
Do not use whole-engine cargo green as this cut's meter.

TARGET project/residency.rs ← validity discipline from condemned engines/segments.rs
TARGET project/current.rs ← segment cache/serving from condemned engines/segments.rs
TARGET kyzo-model/src/parse/search.rs ← pure-data FTS AST from condemned engines/text/ast.rs

TARGET project/gazetteer.rs ← battery from condemned engines/gazetteer_hostile.rs

proof_meters: compile-fail by signature: witness_after_snapshot without the open snapshot argument
compile-fail: crate-consumer cannot construct FtsExpr outside the parser's bounded constructor

crates/kyzo-core/src/project/contract.rs.kind: TARGET — the projection contract: rebuildable, typed corruption, one read boundary
ban: raw DecodeError leaking as an engine's contract
carried_notes: Corruption-is-an-error-never-a-panic extended to every index read path, defined ONCE because all engines name it; the downcast discipline separates codec corruption (the index's own) from storage/IO errors (pass through unchanged).
'The index can be dropped and re-created from its base relation' is the projection law this file states.

ban: cross-process watermark claims (fresh process = zero + empty cache)
durable watermark organ (carried obligation names §20's answer)
proof_meter: compile-fail by signature: witness before snapshot
carried_notes: Monotone, process-local; fresh process = zero + empty cache so cross-process staleness cannot arise.
bump_before_commit / witness_after_snapshot pairing was adopted after a hostile review proved the documented-ordering version racy — soundness by SIGNATURE, not calling convention.
should_build: N consecutive misses at the SAME witness; miss-map loss only delays rebuild, never corrupts serving — the never-a-source-of-truth claim is PROVEN by a loss test.
Day-one note (carried obligation): if projections ever persist, the generation vocabulary becomes durable under §20 (Store commit-order position) with §35 two-axis keyspaces.

crates/kyzo-core/src/project/current.rs.kind: TARGET — which projection build is live for readers right now
ban: serving on anything but witness equality
carried_notes: Witness equality is the entire serving criterion; Arc-held orphans serve mid-scan readers to completion; harmless-early-orphan rollback story.
Declining-is-always-sound: the u32 decline and the gate decline are one doctrine — a projection is optional speed; the fallback pays no more than the build would have.
Dense row-major values + u32 END offsets; prefix_range/partition binary search; checked_row_end factored out to be testable without 4.3 billion values.
Segments::OFF threading is door plumbing #120 replaces (carried note).

crates/kyzo-model/src/parse/search.rs.kind: TARGET (map seat; parse zone not yet landed in kyzo-model) — FTS mini-language + the AST it produces
proof_meter: compile-fail: FtsExpr constructed outside the parser bound
delete_meter: rg -n 'FtsExpr' crates/kyzo-model/src/parse/search.rs → definition present; zero definitions in kyzo-core
carried_notes: THE DEPTH INVARIANT doc travels verbatim: the parser is the only non-test constructor and bounds depth/operator count; every recursive walk including compiler-derived Drop/Clone/PartialEq/Hash is stack-safe BECAUSE the bound holds; 'a new constructor must either enforce the same bound or make every walk, including Drop, iterative' — the sharpest derived-Drop stack-safety analysis in the tree; bounding at the parser is proven STRONGER than an iterative rewrite.
PREFIX literals pass through whole (meaning argument); shallow-is_empty with flatten-as-normalizer stated as a design pair.

crates/kyzo-core/src/project/text/ast.rs.kind: TARGET — analyzer-coupled tokenize extension over the model FTS AST
ban: redefining the FTS AST types (model owns them)
delete_meter: rg -n 'struct FtsExpr|enum FtsExpr' crates/kyzo-core/src/project/text/ast.rs → zero (imports model type)

crates/kyzo-core/src/project/sparse/spar.kind: TARGET — named hostile-review battery beside the sparse projection
owns: eight adversarial sparse tests + fixture plumbing + run_bits exact f32 bit projection
carried_notes: Battery targets exactly what the module's own tests under-pin (the admission sort under summation_order_is_pinned fed a pre-sorted query); independent f64 dot-product reference on exact-representable weights; large tie-set top-k survivors pinned (ascending-key tiebreak + truncation); denormal/huge weights finite and deterministic — +inf a lawful score, NaN never; -0.0 admitted and never a hit; k=0 check-before-push regression pin (twin owed beside fts_search — carried obligation).

crates/kyzo-core/src/project/gazetteer.r.kind: TARGET living seat — production engine (landed) + absorbed hostile battery as test submodule
owns: gazetteer hostile-review test section (ref_tag, assert_agree, Turkish dotted/dotless i, ligature/combining-mark, nesting/overlap, 4000-case seeded fuzz, İNDEX mutation differential, two-compiles agreement)
ban: collapsing the two structurally different oracles into one
carried_notes: TWO structurally different oracles for one engine (greedy scan vs candidate enumeration + greedy resumption) — agreement between three independent derivations is the strongest law here; the mutation-differential pattern: a test justified by the exact mutant it kills (to_ascii_lowercase → to_lowercase diverges on İNDEX); every span boundary-truthful and slicing back to its surface; determinism on re-tag.

crates/kyzo-trials/src/fuzz.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — corrupt-dictionary sweep re-laned from #[ignore]

crates/kyzo-core/benches/bench_api.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — phase-timing scaling probe re-laned to the bench lane
delete_meter: rg -n 'gazetteer' crates/kyzo-core/benches → probe present in the bench lane

must_add: kyzo-model/src/parse/search.rs description notes it owns the FTS AST pure-data types (FtsExpr/FtsLiteral/FtsNear)

final_meters: project/{contract,residency,current}.rs and project/text/ast.rs and project/sparse/sparse_hostile.rs each exceed stub length
FtsExpr defined in kyzo-model only; project/text extends it
#82 regression battery present under project/residency.rs tests
carried_obligations recorded at their seats (durable-generation note; k=0 twin; re-laned probes; Segments::OFF note)

seated_laws.law: a projection serves only on witness equality; disagreement rebuilds or refuses (§51)
seated_laws.meter: witness-equality-governs-service battery present and green (orphan + evict + held Arc arms)

seated_laws.law: index-read corruption is a typed error at one boundary, never a panic
exact-bit score determinism — byte identity, not tolerance
seated_laws.seat: project/sparse/sparse_hostile.rs (house standard for all score-emitting projections)
seated_laws.meter: run_bits-style assertions compare f32 bit patterns, not approximate equality

qae.q: target-arch carries BOTH project/text/*.rs analyzer files AND a nested project/text/tokenizer/*.rs subtree with the same filenames (its own line admits 'target layout may collapse with text/'). Which layout does this cut treat as law when landing engines/text material?
qae.a: Treat the flat project/text/ layout as law and the nested tokenizer/ subtree as a map redundancy to be collapsed: one file per analyzer at project/text/, no duplicate subtree. This cut lands ast.rs at the flat seat; the map's duplicate lines are a recorded map-honesty correction for the operator's next map pass.
qae.e: Two seats for one tokenizer is two homes for one truth — the map's own hedge line shows it was never a ruling. Landing into both would mint twins; landing into nested-only contradicts every already-landed flat file. Close: map pass deletes the tokenizer/ duplicate lines; meter — no filename appears twice under project/text/ in target-arch.

campaigns_proposed.campaign: wipe-projection campaign: for each landed projection (fts, hnsw, lsh, sparse, spatial, gazetteer), delete the projection state under a live base relation, then query — assert rebuild-from-base or typed refuse; any silent wrong answer falsifies the rebuildable-cache claim.
